sections_cited: section 21 of the Sales Tax Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 4451 of 2025 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection 2. For hearing of CMA No.18468/2025 3. For hearing of main case 06.10.2025 Mr. Darvesh K. Mandan, advocate for the petitioner Mr. Mukesh Kumar Khatri, advocate for the respondent Ms. Zahrah Sahar Vayani, Assistant Attorney General Learned counsel for the respondent submits that the petitioner is aggrieved of the blacklisting order dated 07.05.2025, however, the remedy per section 21 of the Sales Tax Act, 1990 is no longer available. Learned counsel states that the petitioner may file an appeal before the Tribunal within a period of one week and the same shall be decided vide a speaking order after providing an opportunity of hearing to the petitioner. Learned counsel further states that pending the aforesaid no further coercive action shall be taken against the petitioner and the Appellate Tribunal shall preferably decide the matter within one month. Learned counsel for the petitioner has no cavil to the aforesaid and states that the petition may be disposed of in the aforesaid terms. Order accordingly. Judge Judge B-K Soomro
